home_title: Not sure where to start with Kubernetes?
home_subtitle: The flight simulator for Kubernetes. Prepare for the CKA with courses and drills in your browser.
home_features_speed_description: Load the page and you're good to go: no waiting, no setup
home_features_stability_description: Everything runs locally: stay in the flow, no interruptions
home_features_price_description: No cloud costs to cover: learn without breaking the bank

changelog_metaDescription: KubeMastery product changelog: recent updates and improvements.
termsOfService_intro: Welcome to KubeMastery. By accessing or using our platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully. For details on how we process personal data and analytics usage data, please also review our Privacy Policy.
termsOfService_acceptance_content: By accessing and using KubeMastery, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our service.
termsOfService_description_content: KubeMastery is an online learning platform that provides Kubernetes training through a browser-based cluster simulator. We offer courses, tutorials, and hands-on practice environments.
termsOfService_account_content: To access certain features, you may need to create an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account.
termsOfService_use_content: You agree not to use the service to: (a) violate any laws or regulations; (b) infringe upon the rights of others; (c) transmit any harmful or malicious code; (d) attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems; (e) interfere with or disrupt the service.
termsOfService_intellectual_content: All content on KubeMastery, including but not limited to text, graphics, logos, and software, is the property of KubeMastery or its content suppliers and is protected by copyright and other intellectual property laws.
termsOfService_limitation_content: KubeMastery is provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind. We shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.
termsOfService_changes_content: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. We will notify users of any material changes by updating the 'Last updated' date. Your continued use of the service constitutes acceptance of the modified terms.
termsOfService_refund_content: Payments are processed by Paddle (paddle.com), our Merchant of Record. Refunds are provided at the sole discretion of Paddle on a case-by-case basis and may be refused. If you are a Consumer, you have the right to cancel within 14 days of purchase without giving any reason, unless the digital content has already been accessed or downloaded. There are no refunds on unused subscription periods. If you purchased using a free trial, you must cancel before the trial period expires to avoid being charged. To request a refund, please contact Paddle directly at paddle.com.
termsOfService_contact_content: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us through our contact page.
contact_intro: For support, or general questions, send us a message. We do our best to reply quickly.
contact_success: Your message has been sent successfully.
contact_error_default: Unable to send your message right now.
privacyPolicy_intro: At KubeMastery, we are committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your personal data, including certain usage data related to product analytics.
privacyPolicy_collection_content: We collect information you provide directly, such as your email address and authentication-related information (for example through magic link or an OAuth provider). We also collect usage and performance data (for example page views, interactions, journey events, browser/device technical information, and diagnostics) to understand how the platform is used. In some cases, we may enable session recordings (session replay) to analyze UX or technical issues. We do not intentionally send sensitive data in analytics events, and users should not submit sensitive data in free-text fields.
privacyPolicy_use_content: We use this information to provide and improve the service, personalize your learning experience, track progress, secure your account, communicate with you about your account, and send important service-related updates. Analytics and session replay data are used to measure usage, identify friction points, troubleshoot incidents, and improve the product. Depending on your jurisdiction and processing activity, the legal basis may be your consent or our legitimate interest in operating, securing, and improving the service.
privacyPolicy_security_content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information. Your data is stored securely and we use encryption to protect sensitive information.
privacyPolicy_storage_content: We use browser local storage to keep certain session data and preferences (such as theme settings). This data remains on your device. Some account and progress data is also processed server-side to provide authentication and continuity of your learning experience. You can clear local data at any time in your browser settings.
privacyPolicy_sharing_content: We do not sell your personal information. We may share limited data only with technical service providers necessary to operate the platform (for example authentication, hosting, and infrastructure providers), with your consent, to comply with legal obligations, or to protect our rights and platform security. This includes our product analytics provider PostHog for usage events and, when enabled, session replay. We configure this service for EU data residency when that option is available.
privacyPolicy_rights_content: You may request access, correction, deletion, or a copy of your personal data. You may also object to certain analytics processing, request restriction of processing, and withdraw consent where consent is the legal basis. You can exercise these rights through your account settings or through our contact page. We respond within a reasonable timeframe in line with applicable law.
privacyPolicy_retention_content: We retain personal data while your account is active and for a limited period afterward when required for legal, accounting, security, or dispute-resolution purposes. Analytics and session replay data are retained for a limited, proportionate period for measurement, security, and product improvement purposes, then deleted or anonymized according to our internal retention policy.
privacyPolicy_changes_content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes by updating the 'Last updated' date. Your continued use of the service constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.
privacyPolicy_contact_content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, our analytics practices, or to exercise your rights, please contact us through our contact page.
pricing_title: Practice real Kubernetes commands for free during early access
pricing_discount_intro: Student, unemployed, or on a tight budget?
pricing_discount_dialog_paragraph_1: We believe education should be accessible to everyone, regardless of financial situation. If the full price is a genuine barrier for you, the reduced rate is there for you, no proof required.
pricing_discount_dialog_paragraph_2: Thanks for choosing KubeMastery to learn Kubernetes.
pricing_checkout_dialog_description: Enter your email to open the checkout page.
pricing_checkout_invalid_email_error: Please enter a valid email address.
pricing_faq_a1: A Kubernetes cluster simulator runs entirely in your browser. You practice kubectl commands and manage resources in real time, no real cluster or cloud. Because everything is local, loading is instant and there’s no network latency or server wait.
pricing_faq_q2: Does it resemble a real cluster?
pricing_faq_a2: Yes. We test against the OpenAPI spec and build our courses from the official Kubernetes docs, so behavior matches real clusters as closely as possible. But Kubernetes is a complex system so some commands may behave differently, especially those that are not in the course yet.
pricing_faq_q3: Do I need prior experience with Kubernetes?
pricing_faq_a3: No. The courses start from scratch. Basic Linux command-line familiarity is helpful but not required. We cover the essentials as you go.
pricing_faq_q4: Does it prepare me for the CKA or CKAD exam?
pricing_faq_a4: That's the goal. The simulator reproduces the terminal-only exam environment and the courses are built from the official Kubernetes documentation. The common foundation is complete. Full CKA and CKAD preparation is a work in progress: new lessons are added every day. We are not affiliated with the Linux Foundation, and the exam voucher is not included.
pricing_faq_q6: What kind of support do you offer?
pricing_faq_a6: Support is by email only. There is no 24/7 hotline. As a solo-built project, response time may vary. I do my best to reply to everyone within a reasonable delay.
pricing_faq_a7: Not yet. The platform is currently closed source. Opening the lesson content is something I am considering. For the cluster simulator itself, no decision has been made.
pricing_faq_a8: Yes, partially. AI is used to help write course content and code. Everything is reviewed, verified, and corrected before being published.
pricing_faq_a9: Yes. Every purchase includes a 30-day money-back guarantee. Contact us within 30 days of payment and we will refund you in full.
courses_subtitle: Learn Kubernetes step by step with structured paths
home_stats_subtitle: Kubernetes is here to stay: massive adoption, a booming market, and trusted by thousands of companies
home_stats_adoption_description: 66% of organizations use Kubernetes in production, with another 18% evaluating it
home_stats_market_description: From $2.61 billion in 2025 to $14.6 billion by 2033, growing at 24% annually
home_stats_companies_description: Trusted by over 50,000 companies worldwide to power their production workloads
home_features_title: Learning Kubernetes doesn’t have to be hard.
home_features_subtitle: Local setup is a hassle. Cloud is expensive. Practice in your browser instead
home_concerns_section1_intro: Pilots and astronauts train in simulators before the real thing. This is the same idea for Kubernetes. A lightweight JavaScript implementation of core Kubernetes behavior with a built-in terminal.
home_concerns_section1_point2: Visualizer to see the Kubernetes cluster
home_fit_yes_1: I'm new to Kubernetes and don't know where to start
home_fit_yes_2: I want to learn Kubernetes without setting up a home lab
home_fit_yes_4: I'm a visual learner and want to see the cluster while I practice
home_fit_yes_5: I'm not ready to invest in expensive cloud labs yet
home_fit_no_1: I already know Kubernetes and need advanced topics
home_fit_no_2: I'm looking for a CKA or CKAD exam simulator
home_fit_no_3: I need hands-on practice with a real local cluster
home_fit_no_3_extra: using kind alongside the course is a great combo
home_fit_no_4: I'm looking for a full DevOps course
home_guarantee_subtitle: We believe you will learn something valuable. If not, we will refund you.
home_reliability_title: It feels real. Here is why.
home_reliability_subtitle: Not magic. Not guesswork. Just careful testing.
home_reliability_conformance_title: Tested against real kubectl output, every space included
home_reliability_conformance_description: We run the exact same commands on a real cluster and compare every line of output with the simulator. Spacing, column alignment, exit codes. All of it.
home_reliability_conformance_alt: Conformance test output diff between Kubernetes and simulator
home_reliability_openapi_description: Every resource the simulator creates is validated against official Kubernetes OpenAPI specs. Same fields, same shapes, no surprises when you move to the real thing.
home_terminal_limit_message: Like what you see? Create an account to unlock the full experience 🙂
modules_subtitle: Learn at your own pace by choosing the topics that interest you
supported_subtitle: The full list of kubectl commands, Kubernetes resources and shell tools available in the simulator and what is planned next.
supported_supported_intro: Open the terminal and try any of these right now.
supported_planned_intro: These areas are part of the roadmap and prioritized to expand CKA coverage and ecosystem skills.
supported_prereq_intro: A guided prerequisite path to make sure you have the right base before deeper Kubernetes practice.
supported_prereq_linux_text: Terminal navigation, files, permissions, processes, and core CLI habits used every day with kubectl.
supported_prereq_docker_text: Images, containers, networking and runtime concepts that make Kubernetes behavior easier to understand.
supported_prereq_kubernetes_text: Start Kubernetes with stronger foundations and focus on architecture, operations and troubleshooting.
supported_transparency_note: KubeMastery is a local simulator with a progressive roadmap. We show explicit support coverage so you always know what to expect.
supported_cta_text: Want to train with what is already available today?
